acceptance_criteria.text: The app keeps the existing project/session navigation and, when a session is selected, renders a dedicated session workspace area next to the session list instead of showing only the list. If no session is selected, the workspace area shows an explicit empty state.

proof.summary: Selected sessions render inside a dedicated `session-panel-area` next to the sidebar.
An explicit empty state is shown when no session is selected.
Playwright coverage asserts the workspace area and empty/session-selected states.
Targeted usage-recordings tests passed.

acceptance_criteria.text: The selected-session workspace adopts a brutalist/minimalist visual treatment for the session area and its panels: no card-within-card presentation, no rounded corners, no decorative shadows, minimal padding, and separation primarily through `border-border`, `divide-y`, and small gaps.

proof.summary: Panel shells are flat bordered sections without rounded card styling or shadows.
Global radius token is set to zero.
CodeMirror editor rounding is removed.
xterm viewport rounding is removed.
Panel controls use flat borders and tight gaps instead of nested cards.

gaps: Brutalist/minimal styling is primarily proven by source review; this refresh did not add a fresh screenshot artifact.

acceptance_criteria.text: The session header remains the single place for session/runtime status and start-stop controls, including the existing session/OpenCode/Docker context needed to understand launch state; that information is not duplicated inside the Agent panel body.

proof.summary: The session header now contains runtime context, badges, and controls.
Sandbox mode and Docker readiness are surfaced in the header.
Start and stop controls live in the header.
Agent panel fallback states do not duplicate runtime controls.
The running-state Agent panel renders only the chat iframe surface.
Playwright coverage checks the agent chat surface after startup.

acceptance_criteria.text: Open session panels render as a single horizontally scrollable, non-wrapping strip, each open panel fills the available session-area height, and each panel provides its own internal scrolling for overflowing content rather than forcing the full page to grow vertically.

proof.summary: The session panel rail is `flex-nowrap` with horizontal overflow enabled.
Each panel wrapper is fixed-width, full-height, and shrink-safe.
Agent panel content keeps overflow internal.
Files panel tree scrolls internally.
Editor tabs scroll internally within the Editor panel.
Git file list scrolls within the Git panel.
Browser content remains inside its own overflow-hidden panel body.
Targeted Playwright coverage revalidated horizontal rail behavior.

gaps: Internal scrolling is source-verified for several panels rather than asserted separately for every panel in Playwright.

acceptance_criteria.text: When a session is first shown in the new layout, only the Agent panel is open by default; other session panels are not auto-opened, and the user can open them on demand through explicit UI controls without displacing the default Agent panel.

proof.summary: Default session panels are defined as agent-only.
Active session panel state falls back to the default agent-only set.
Agent is forcibly pinned into the open panel list if missing.
Other panels open through explicit toggle buttons in the header strip.
Playwright confirmed only the Agent panel is open by default.

acceptance_criteria.text: The Agent panel is a chat UI for the existing OpenCode server for that session, using the already-started server/runtime flow rather than a brand-new backend chat protocol, and the panel no longer renders the current status-card-and-log-console layout as its primary UI.

proof.summary: The frontend derives the chat URL from the existing session agent runtime port.
The active Agent panel receives the existing runtime-derived chat URL.
The Agent panel embeds the OpenCode UI via iframe instead of the old log console.
Backend still starts OpenCode through the existing serve/server runtime flow.
No new frontend chat API contract was added to the Big IDE types.
Playwright verified the agent chat surface is present after startup.

gaps: The iframe approach assumes the OpenCode web UI continues to be served from the reported root URL and remains embeddable.

acceptance_criteria.text: Clicking a file in the Files panel opens that file in the existing Editor panel when one is already open, or opens/creates the standard Editor panel when none is open, and activates a tab for the clicked file in that editor surface.

proof.summary: File rows expose stable selectors and call the open-file handler on click.
Opening a file ensures the Editor panel is present for the active session.
`openFile` loads or reuses file buffers before activating the tab.
Editor tabs render from per-session open file state with an active tab marker.
Playwright verified Files -> Editor open flow and active tab activation.

acceptance_criteria.text: Session selection remains the driver for panel content, file tree context, and agent/editor state, so switching the selected session updates the visible workspace area to the new session without changing backend API contracts or session identity handling.

proof.summary: Visible workspace data is derived from the current `activeSession`.
Editor state is memoized per active session from session-keyed storage.
Per-session editor buffers are stored and updated by session id.
Session-row clicks continue to set the active project/session and drive the workspace.
No backend contract changes are present in the current task diff.

gaps: This refresh relies on source review plus passing flow tests; it does not add a dedicated multi-session-switch Playwright assertion.

acceptance_criteria.text: Playwright end-to-end coverage is added or updated to verify the selected-session workspace area, the default agent-only state, horizontal panel scrolling, Docker-backed sandbox startup path where Docker is available, the Agent chat surface, and the Files-to-Editor open-file flow using stable selectors/assertions.

proof.summary: The usage-recordings suite covers session lifecycle and workspace rendering.
A dedicated case exercises the Docker-requested startup path and agent chat surface.
A dedicated case covers Files -> Editor tabs plus Git/Browser panel usability.
Docker was available in the evidence environment.
All 3 targeted Playwright tests passed.

gaps: The Docker Playwright case requests docker mode but still tolerates runtime fallback to host mode if the runtime does not stay on docker.

known_gaps: The Docker-specific Playwright scenario is smoke coverage because it tolerates runtime fallback to host mode.
The styling verdict is source-based and was not paired with a fresh screenshot artifact in this refresh.
